items.before: The current seed (post-loophole-fix in a4723f8) describes the convention as if compliance were optional. It says 'STOP' / 'do NOT run apply_patch' but doesn't tell codex that the runtime will actually deny the call if it tries anyway.
items.after: Add one short paragraph after the gate section: 'A PreToolUse hook is installed in this codex config. If your first action on a multi-file task is apply_patch / Edit / Write / Bash without a covering worklist item, the hook will return permissionDecision:deny and your tool call will be blocked with the message: Tool call blocked by PreToolUse hook: <reason>. The hook is enforcement, the convention above is the cooperative path. Follow the convention and your work succeeds; ignore it and you will see deny messages until you propose first.' Light reinforcement since the UserPromptSubmit injection already conveys this at every change-request turn. NOTE: this item is now stale — codex-startup-instructions.md was rewritten as part of codex-developer-instructions-investigation, so the 'before' state no longer exists and the proposed paragraph would no-op against the new content. Drop this item unless the user wants to add a different acknowledgment to the rewritten file.
